name: game_start
description: The game begins. The player wakes up in the mystery room.
consequence: You are in a cold stone room. There is a wooden table in the center, a painting on the far wall, and a heavy door with no visible handle. A faint smell of dust and something older than dust.

name: inspect_room
description: The player looks around the room, examines their surroundings, or tries to get a general sense of the space.
consequence: You take in the room properly. Stone walls, no windows. A wooden table sits in the center with something on it. A large painting hangs crooked on the far wall. The door behind you is solid iron with a keypad beside it — nine digits, backlit faintly red.

name: find_note
description: The player examines, approaches, or looks at the table or whatever is on it.
consequence: On the table is a folded piece of paper, yellowed with age. The handwriting inside is cramped and urgent: 'Do not ignore what watches from the wall. The answer lives behind the eye that does not blink.' The ink at the bottom has been smudged — as if someone pressed the paper against something wet, or wept.

name: find_painting
description: The player looks at, moves toward, or examines the painting on the wall.
consequence: It is an oil painting — a portrait of a young man with hollow eyes that seem to track you as you move. The frame is dusty but the canvas looks oddly preserved. The painting hangs slightly off-center, as if it has been moved and replaced many times.

name: read_note
description: The player reads the note, picks it up to read it, or tries to decipher the writing.
consequence: As you finish the last line, the air drops sharply in temperature. The faint glow from the walls dims for a moment. Then — nothing. Silence. Until a sound like paper tearing, and a figure is simply there, standing in the corner. Translucent, still, watching you with hollow pale eyes. The ghost. It was not there a moment ago.

name: inspect_painting_closely
description: The player looks behind the painting, moves it, lifts it, or investigates the area around it closely.
consequence: You tilt the frame away from the wall. Behind it, carved directly into the stone, is a small circular indentation — a button, or something meant to be pressed. It is cold to the touch and slightly recessed, as if it has been pressed before but not recently.

name: talk_to_ghost
description: The player speaks to, greets, addresses, or attempts to communicate with the ghost in any way.

name: ask_ghost_about_button
description: The player asks the ghost specifically about the button, the indentation behind the painting, how to use it, what it does, or how to escape.

name: ask_ghost_for_code
description: The player asks the ghost for the code, the numbers, the combination, or what to enter into the keypad.

name: push_button
description: The player pushes, presses, or activates the button hidden behind the painting.
consequence: You press the button. A low mechanical hum reverberates through the stone. The red backlight on the keypad beside the door shifts to amber. Something has changed — the keypad is now waiting for input.

name: enter_code
description: The player enters a code, types numbers into the keypad, or attempts to use the keypad on the door.
consequence: Your fingers press 4-4-7-1. A pause — one breath held — and then a deep mechanical clunk as the lock disengages.
